sectionsUnavailable: The authenticated Athletic Performance detail page does not expose a direct reportFile sample report.
No calibrated athletic-performance, training-response, injury-risk, VO2 max, sport-suitability, or ancestry-applicability model is supplied with this fixture.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived ACTN3 rs1815739 CT call is available. This is trait context only and does not predict athletic ability, strength, speed, endurance, recovery, injury risk, VO2 max, or sport fit.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: ACTN3 is one of the best-studied genes in athletic-performance research, but performance is shaped by many genetic and non-genetic factors.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived ACE ID context row is available. This is not enough to choose sports, prescribe workouts, estimate injury risk, or predict training response.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: ACE has been discussed in performance research, but environment, training history, health, and many other factors remain important.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated athletic-performance score, training-response model, injury-risk model, VO2 max model, sport-suitability model, or ancestry-applicability metadata is supplied with this fixture.
resultRows.sourceLabel: NHGRI polygenic risk scores / GWAS Catalog
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The local report can describe supplied marker context, but it should not quantify performance, training, injury, VO2 max, or sport-fit probabilities without a validated model.

resultRows.item: RunDNA public sample PDF athletic/endurance rows
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public RunDNA sample PDF shows methodology boundaries, an endurance-performance marker table, and a response-to-endurance-training marker table that can be used as Athletic Performance sibling output-structure examples.
resultRows.sourceLabel: RunDNA Endurance Performance Plan sample report
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: These rows document a related Sequencing.com/Toolbox Genomics report format only. They must not be treated as this local fixture's athletic ability, training response, injury risk, VO2 max, diet, supplement, medical-clearance, or sport-selection conclusion.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample explains that recommendations are chosen for actionability, are based on SNP evaluation and human studies, and are educational rather than intended to diagnose or treat disease.
sampleRows.description: The introduction also says health depends on genetics, diet, exercise, sleep, environment, and other traits, and recommends talking to a healthcare provider before changing diet, supplement use, or exercise regimen.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Athletic Performance sibling output-structure evidence. Extracted from pages 2-3 of the public RunDNA sample PDF; use as boundary and output-format structure only, not local health, training, supplement, or exercise advice.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Better Endurance Performance page says people with similar genetic markers may experience better performance in endurance exercise and shows ADRB2 rs1042713 GG non-variant, ADRB2 rs1042714 CG variant, COL5A1 rs12722 CC non-variant, ACE rs1799752 TTG non-variant, PPARA rs4253778 GG variant, CKMM rs8111989 TT variant, NRF2 rs12594956 AC variant, and PPARGC1A rs8192678 TC variant.
sampleRows.description: The page includes endurance-performance condition text and gene definitions for ADRB2, COL5A1, ACE, and PPARA.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Athletic Performance sibling output-structure evidence. Extracted from page 12 of the public RunDNA sample PDF; this is observed sample output structure and not local endurance ability, performance, training, or medical advice.

sampleRows.item: Response to Endurance Training gene table
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Response to Endurance Training page says people with similar genetic markers may experience lower or delayed cardiorespiratory response to endurance exercise and shows AMPD1 rs17602729 GG non-variant.
sampleRows.description: The page includes condition-definition text describing endurance training and an AMPD1 gene definition.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Athletic Performance sibling output-structure evidence. Extracted from page 18 of the public RunDNA sample PDF; this is observed sample output structure and not local training response, capacity, or VO2 max advice.

trainingContext: Use this report as educational athletic-performance genetics context, not as a training plan or sport-selection tool.
Keep the public RunDNA sibling sample rows separate from local findings; sample genotypes and labels are format examples only.
Discuss training, injury prevention, recovery, and performance goals with qualified coaches or health professionals.

missingInputs: No direct Athletic Performance sample report file is exposed in the authenticated detail-page artifact.
No calibrated athletic-performance probability model is supplied.
No validated training-response, injury-risk, VO2 max, recovery, sport-suitability, or ancestry-applicability model is supplied.
Most known and unknown genetic and non-genetic contributors to athletic performance are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Athletic performance is a complex trait influenced by genetics, training, environment, opportunity, health, and many other factors.
The RunDNA sample rows are sibling output-structure evidence only, not personal Athletic Performance findings.
A local agent should not quantify performance probability without a calibrated model and applicability metadata.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not predict elite ability, prescribe training, estimate injury risk, quantify VO2 max, or recommend sport selection.
A single or small number of markers cannot explain athletic performance.
